items.title: Chrome secretly installs a 4GB AI model on Windows 11 – here’s how to stop it
items.description: Stop Chrome from silently downloading the 4GB Gemini Nano model on Windows 11. Use this Registry fix to block it for good.
items.titleOriginal: Chrome secretly installs a 4GB AI model on Windows 11 – here’s how to stop it
items.content:
@@@cardlink
url: https://pocketables.com/2026/05/chrome-secretly-installed-u2s-songs-of-innocence-without-your-consent-and-how-to-uninstall-4gb-ai-in-chrome.html
title: "Chrome secretly installed U2's Songs of Innocence without your consent and how to uninstall (4gb AI in Chrome) - Pocketables"
description: "There's been a lot of talk about where a 4gb AI file on people's computers came from and how Chrome secretly pushed it... much like they secretly pushed"
host: pocketables.com
favicon: https://pocketables.com/wp-content/uploads/2010/05/cropped-cropped-Pocketables_logo_400x400-1-32x32.jpg
image: https://pocketables.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/image-3.png
@@@

@@@cardlink
url: https://wil.to/posts/googles-prompt-api/
title: "Google’s Prompt API"
description: "No web standard should require you to agree to an advertising company’s “terms of use.”"
host: wil.to
favicon: https://wil.to/favicon.ico
image: https://wil.to/assets/og-images/googles-prompt-api-preview.jpeg
@@@

@@@cardlink
url: https://www.thatprivacyguy.com/blog/chrome-silent-nano-install/
title: "Google Chrome silently installs a 4 GB AI model on your device without consent. At a billion-device scale the climate costs are insane. — That Privacy Guy!"
description: "Google Chrome is downloading a 4 GB Gemini Nano model onto users' machines without consent, with no opt-in, no opt-out short of enterprise tooling, and an automatic re-download every time the user deletes it. The pattern is identical to the Anthropic Claude Desktop case I wrote about last month, but the scale is between two and three orders of magnitude larger. This article does the legal analysis and, for the first time, the environmental analysis. The numbers are not small."
host: www.thatprivacyguy.com
image: https://www.thatprivacyguy.com/blog/assets/2026-05-04-001.png
@@@

items.title: Install web apps with the new HTML install element
items.description: Installing web apps has always required JavaScript. When you use the `beforeinstallprompt` event, the installation flow lives entirely in script. The new `<install>` element changes that: drop a single HTML element into your page and the browser renders a trusted install button for you, with no JavaScript required.
items.titleOriginal: Install web apps with the new HTML install element  |  Blog  |  Chrome for Developers

items.title: Media Queries Range Syntax
items.description: Media queries are the backbone of responsive design. We use them to control how a design should change based on the viewport size. But the `min-width` and `max-width` syntax can be confusing, and in some cases, cause layout bugs that take time to spot. This article aims to convince you to use range queries, starting today.
